ai-w6-t1.answer: AI converts loops to Streams: filter, map, collect. Learn the loop version first — Streams are syntax sugar, not magic.
ai-w6-t1.realWorld: for-loop sum → stream().mapToInt().sum() — same logic, different style.

ai-w6-t2.answer: Lambdas: (x) -> x * 2. Ask AI to explain parameter, arrow, body. Rewrite as anonymous class to compare.

ai-w6-t3.answer: Complex pipeline: ask AI to label each step. Read left-to-right: source → intermediate → terminal.
ai-w6-t3.realWorld: list.stream().filter().map().sorted().collect() — one operation per line for readability.

ai-w6-t4.answer: Inefficient: stream inside loop, repeated sorting, parallel on tiny data. AI flags; you measure.
ai-w6-t4.realWorld: parallelStream() on 10 items — overhead wins nothing.

ai-w6-t5.answer: Thread safety: ask AI when to use synchronized, ConcurrentHashMap, atomic classes. Race conditions need reproduction.
ai-w6-t5.realWorld: counter++ on shared int across threads — broken; AtomicInteger fixes.

ai-w6-t6.answer: Concurrency test ideas: multiple threads, same resource, assert final count. Flaky tests need careful design.
ai-w6-t6.realWorld: AI suggests thread pool test; you run 100 iterations to spot races.

ai-w6-t7.answer: Profile before parallelizing. AI suggests ExecutorService for I/O-bound tasks — not CPU magic on one core.
ai-w6-t7.realWorld: Blocking HTTP calls → thread pool; CPU math → maybe not more threads.

ai-w6-t8.answer: AI may suggest parallelStream everywhere — wrong for small data, shared state, or ordered requirements.
ai-w6-t8.realWorld: Order-sensitive pipeline + parallel = subtle bugs — stick to sequential first.
